sentences: Twin Peaks, also known as Twin Peaks: The Return, is an American mystery drama television revival series created by Mark Frost and David Lynch.
It is a continuation of the 1990–91 ABC series of the same name.
The limited series consists of 18 episodes and premiered on Showtime on May 21, 2017, following a world premiere on May 19, 2017, at The Theatre at Ace Hotel.
The series was developed and written by Lynch and Frost over several years and directed by Lynch.
An ensemble of returning and new cast members appear, led by original star Kyle MacLachlan.

FBI Special Agent Dale Bartholomew Cooper, portrayed by Kyle MacLachlan, is a fictional character and the protagonist of the ABC television series "Twin Peaks" and Showtime's continuation of the same name.
He also plays a supporting role in the prequel film "".
Audrey Horne is a fictional character from the ABC television series "Twin Peaks", played by Sherilyn Fenn.
The character was created by David Lynch.
She was introduced in the pilot.

The daughter of Ben (Richard Beymer) and Sylvia Horne, sister of Johnny Horne (Robert Bauer) and half-sister of Donna Hayward (Lara Flynn Boyle), her storylines focused on her infatuation with the series protagonist Dale Cooper (Kyle MacLachlan), infiltrating the brothel/casino One Eyed Jacks and becoming an activist through civil disobedience.
Orson Hodge is a fictional character on the ABC television series "Desperate Housewives".
The character is played by Kyle MacLachlan.
Orson is introduced in the final episodes of the second season of the series, and becomes the main mystery of the third season.
MacLachlan left the main cast in season six, but made guest appearances until the eighth and final season.
"Episode 4", also known as "The One-Armed Man", is the fifth episode of the first season of the American mystery television series "Twin Peaks".
The episode was written by Robert Engels, and directed by Tim Hunter.
"Episode 4" features series regulars Kyle MacLachlan, Piper Laurie and Richard Beymer, and introduces series co-creator David Lynch in the role of Gordon Cole.
"Episode 5", also known as "Cooper's Dreams", is the sixth episode of the first season of the American mystery television series "Twin Peaks".
The episode was written by series co-creator Mark Frost and directed by Lesli Linka Glatter.
"Episode 5" features series regulars Kyle MacLachlan, Michael Ontkean and Richard Beymer, with guest appearances by Chris Mulkey and David Patrick Kelly.
"Blue Velvet" is a 1986 American mystery film written and directed by David Lynch.
The movie exhibits elements of both film noir and surrealism.
The film features Kyle MacLachlan, Isabella Rossellini, Dennis Hopper, and Laura Dern.
The title is taken from the 1963 Bobby Vinton song of the same name, which is featured in the film.
Although initially detested by some mainstream critics, the film has now become widely acclaimed.
Kyle Merritt MacLachlan ( ; born February 22, 1959) is an American actor.

MacLachlan is best known for his role as Dale Cooper in "Twin Peaks" (1990–1991; 2017) and its film prequel "" (1992), as well as roles in two of David Lynch's films: Paul Atreides in "Dune" (1984) and Jeffrey Beaumont in "Blue Velvet" (1986).

MacLachlan's other film roles include Lloyd Gallagher in "The Hidden" (1987), Ray Manzarek in "The Doors" (1991), Clifford Vandercave in "The Flintstones" (1994), Zack Carey in "Showgirls" (1995), and the voice of Riley's dad in "Inside Out" (2015).
Valerie "Val" Cooper is a fictional character appearing in American comic books published by Marvel Comics.
The character works for the Office of National Emergency.
She is most notable for her work as the liaison for mutant affairs in the United States government.

She once claimed to have been inspired to government service by the interesting cases her brother, an FBI agent, encountered in his work (it was implied, but never stated outright, that her brother is Dale Cooper from "Twin Peaks").
The pilot episode, also known as Northwest Passage, of the mystery television series "Twin Peaks" premiered on the ABC Network on Sunday, April 8, 1990.
It was written by series creators Mark Frost and David Lynch, and directed by Lynch.

The pilot follows the characters of Dale Cooper and Harry S. Truman as they investigate the death of popular high school student Laura Palmer; Cooper believes the murder has connections to a murder case that occurred a year earlier.
In addition to setting the tone for the show, the episode sets up several character and story arcs and marked the appearance of several recurring characters.
The episode received a strong Nielsen household rating compared to other season one episodes and was well received by fans and critics alike.
The original title for the series was "Northwest Passage", but this was later changed.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

hop1_docs: Kyle MacLachlan | Kyle Merritt MacLachlan ( ; born February 22, 1959) is an American actor.  MacLachlan is best known for his role as Dale Cooper in "Twin Peaks" (1990–1991; 2017) and its film prequel "" (1992), as well as roles in two of David Lynch's films: Paul Atreides in "Dune" (1984) and Jeffrey Beaumont in "Blue Velvet" (1986).  MacLachlan's other film roles include Lloyd Gallagher in "The Hidden" (1987), Ray Manzarek in "The Doors" (1991), Clifford Vandercave in "The Flintstones" (1994), Zack Carey in "Showgirls" (1995), and the voice of Riley's dad in "Inside Out" (2015).

Dale Cooper | FBI Special Agent Dale Bartholomew Cooper, portrayed by Kyle MacLachlan, is a fictional character and the protagonist of the ABC television series "Twin Peaks" and Showtime's continuation of the same name.  He also plays a supporting role in the prequel film "".

Audrey Horne | Audrey Horne is a fictional character from the ABC television series "Twin Peaks", played by Sherilyn Fenn.  The character was created by David Lynch.  She was introduced in the pilot.  The daughter of Ben (Richard Beymer) and Sylvia Horne, sister of Johnny Horne (Robert Bauer) and half-sister of Donna Hayward (Lara Flynn Boyle), her storylines focused on her infatuation with the series protagonist Dale Cooper (Kyle MacLachlan), infiltrating the brothel/casino One Eyed Jacks and becoming an activist through civil disobedience.

Episode 5 (Twin Peaks) | "Episode 5", also known as "Cooper's Dreams", is the sixth episode of the first season of the American mystery television series "Twin Peaks".  The episode was written by series co-creator Mark Frost and directed by Lesli Linka Glatter.  "Episode 5" features series regulars Kyle MacLachlan, Michael Ontkean and Richard Beymer, with guest appearances by Chris Mulkey and David Patrick Kelly.

Episode 3 (Twin Peaks) | "Episode 3", later also known as "Rest in Pain", is the fourth episode of the first season of the American mystery television series "Twin Peaks".  The episode was written by Harley Peyton, and directed by Tina Rathborne.  "Episode 3" features series regulars Kyle MacLachlan, Michael Ontkean and Ray Wise, and introduces Sheryl Lee's second role, Maddy Ferguson.

Episode 4 (Twin Peaks) | "Episode 4", also known as "The One-Armed Man", is the fifth episode of the first season of the American mystery television series "Twin Peaks".  The episode was written by Robert Engels, and directed by Tim Hunter.  "Episode 4" features series regulars Kyle MacLachlan, Piper Laurie and Richard Beymer, and introduces series co-creator David Lynch in the role of Gordon Cole.

The Hidden (film) | The Hidden is an American science fiction action-horror film produced and released in 1987 by New Line Cinema.  The film was written by Bob Hunt (pen name of writer/producer/director Jim Kouf) and directed by Jack Sholder.  The cast features Kyle MacLachlan and Michael Nouri and includes supporting roles by Clu Gulager, Chris Mulkey, Ed O'Ross, Clarence Felder, Claudia Christian and Larry Cedar.  A sequel, "The Hidden II", directed by Seth Pinsker was released in 1993.
response.summary_1: Evidence: MacLachlan is "best known for his role as Dale Cooper in 'Twin Peaks' (1990–1991; 2017)" and Dale Cooper is "the protagonist of the ABC television series 'Twin Peaks' and Showtime's continuation of the same name." Bridge/missing fact: confirm that the 2017 revival referenced above is the "Showtime" continuation (i.e., that the 2017 Twin Peaks series aired on Showtime). Retrieval direction: fetch the "Twin Peaks (2017)" / season 3 or Showtime series page that explicitly states the 2017 revival aired on Showtime.
response.hop2_query: Twin Peaks 2017 Showtime season 3 aired on Showtime
hop2_docs: Twin Peaks (2017 TV series) | Twin Peaks, also known as Twin Peaks: The Return, is an American mystery drama television revival series created by Mark Frost and David Lynch.  It is a continuation of the 1990–91 ABC series of the same name.  The limited series consists of 18 episodes and premiered on Showtime on May 21, 2017, following a world premiere on May 19, 2017, at The Theatre at Ace Hotel.  The series was developed and written by Lynch and Frost over several years and directed by Lynch.  An ensemble of returning and new cast members appear, led by original star Kyle MacLachlan.

Part 8 (Twin Peaks) | "Part 8", also known as "The Return, Part 8", is the eighth episode of "Twin Peaks"' revival series.  It was written by Mark Frost and David Lynch and directed by Lynch.  "Part 8" was broadcast on June 25, 2017, on Showtime and was watched by an audience of 246,000 viewers in the United States.  The episode was composed of lengthy, surreal scenes, with very little dialogue throughout.  It received critical acclaim.

Ray Donovan | Ray Donovan is an American television crime drama series created by Ann Biderman for Showtime.  The twelve-episode first season premiered on June 30, 2013.  The pilot episode broke viewership records, becoming the biggest premiere of all time on Showtime.  Showtime renewed the show for a fourth season, which premiered on June 26, 2016.  On August 11, 2016, Showtime renewed the show for a fifth season, which premiered on August 6, 2017.
Showtime (M*A*S*H) | "Showtime" was the twenty-fourth (and final) episode of the first season of the American television series "M*A*S*H".  It originally aired on March 25, 1973.

Sleeper Cell (TV series) | Sleeper Cell is an American one-hour drama on the Showtime network that began airing on December 4, 2005.  The tagline for the first season was ""Friends.  Neighbors.  Husbands.  Terrorists.""  and the tagline for the second season was ""Cities.  Suburbs.  Airports.  Targets.""  The series was nominated for an Emmy award for Outstanding Miniseries.  The eight-episode second season of the series, titled Sleeper Cell: American Terror, premiered on December 10, 2006.  Both seasons of "Sleeper Cell" were originally aired in an unusual fashion, by filming the entire season ahead of time and then airing the episodes on consecutive nights, such that each brand new season was aired for the first time over a period of less than two weeks.  In Australia, both seasons originally aired on the Showtime Australia channel in 2006/2007.  Re-runs as of 2008 have screened on the showcase channel (part of the Showtime Australia group of channels).

I'm Dying Up Here | I'm Dying Up Here is an American comedy-drama television series created by David Flebotte.  The pilot was written by Flebotte and directed by Jonathan Levine.  It premiered on Showtime on June 4, 2017.  The series is executive produced by Flebotte, Jim Carrey, Michael Aguilar, and Christina Wayne.  It was announced on January 12, 2016, that Showtime had ordered the pilot to series based on the bestselling book by William Knoedelseder.  On September 8, 2017, Showtime renewed the series for a 10-episode second season to premiere in 2018.

NBA Showtime | NBA Showtime is the pregame show aired before each "NBA on NBC" telecast.  The program, a half-hour in length, began during the 1990–91 NBA season, and was initially hosted by Bob Costas.  Costas left in the mid-1990s, and became lead play-by-play voice of "The NBA on NBC" in 1997.  Hannah Storm replaced Costas and hosted "Showtime" until Ahmad Rashād replaced her as host of the pregame show when Storm went on maternity leave in 2001.  Storm returned in 2002 which meant that her and Rashad would alternate as hosts throughout the season.  NBC kept the title of "Showtime" prior to the 2000–01 NBA season.
response.summary_2: The 2017 Twin Peaks revival (featuring Kyle MacLachlan as Dale Cooper) aired on Showtime; it premiered on Showtime on May 21, 2017.
